description: Ansible playbook / role-task invariants. Catches TLS certificate validation turned off on modules, play-level privilege escalation, and shell/command tasks built from Jinja interpolation (command injection). Anchored to YAML key positions so it does not fire on prose. Apply to repos that ship Ansible playbooks or roles.

rules.//: validate_certs: no|false on uri/get_url/yum/apt_repository/etc disables TLS certificate verification, so the module accepts any cert from the remote — a MITM that can swap the downloaded artifact or API response. The `i` flag covers YAML's case variants (no/No/NO, false/False/FALSE). `\b` after the value prevents matching `nope`/`falsey`. HARD: there is no production reason to fetch over unverified TLS.
rules.message: Ansible validate_certs: no/false disables TLS certificate verification on the module (MITM on the fetch). Remove it (the secure default is yes) and, for a private CA, point the module at the CA bundle instead of turning verification off.

rules.//: become: yes/true at PLAY level escalates every task in the play to root. Scoped to playbooks and ad-hoc `.ansible.yml` files only — role task files (`roles/**/tasks/`) are intentionally excluded because `become: yes` at the task level in a role IS the recommended least-privilege pattern (escalate only the tasks that need it). `^\s*become\s*:` (m flag) anchors to a `become` key; `become_user` does not match because of the trailing `_`. WARN.
rules.message: Ansible become: yes at the play level escalates privilege for every task in the play. Prefer task-level `become` on only the tasks that actually need root (least privilege). If play-wide escalation is intentional, confirm it is necessary.

rules.//: A shell:/command: task whose value contains a Jinja `{{ }}` expression interpolates a (often user/inventory-derived) variable straight into a shell command line — classic command injection (`{{ user_input }}` containing `; rm -rf /`). `^\s*(?:ansible\.builtin\.)?(?:shell|command)\s*:` (m flag) anchors to the module key, the optional FQCN prefix covers `ansible.builtin.shell`, and `[^\n]*\{\{` keeps the match on the same line as the key. WARN because some interpolations are over trusted, validated facts — but every one deserves review and, where possible, the args/argv form with `quote` filter. Note: a `command:`/`shell:` block scalar whose Jinja is on a following line is not caught (single-line scope).
rules.message: Ansible shell/command task interpolates a Jinja {{ }} expression into the command line — command injection if the variable is attacker-influenced. Pass untrusted values through the `quote` filter (`{{ var | quote }}`), prefer module args over a shell string, or use a purpose-built module.
